The court grants the request to strike the pleading, which was filed in error (CCP section 473(a)(1)). Plaintiff's counsel will serve all parties with the court's signed order.

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Judgments For Prevailing Party Attorneys Fees On Appeal And In Arbitration Based On The Ruling
Off Calendar Jan-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Calvin Grigsby, Esq. 415-860-6446 of Grigsby & Associates for Petitioner.
Michael Betz, Esq. 415-837-1515 of Allen Matkins for Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. Petitioner Argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Judgments For Prevailing Party Attorney's Fees On Appeal And In Arbitration Based On The Ruling by this Court that the Arbitration based on the Ruling by this Court that the Arbitration is not over and Respondents Extrinsic Fraud in Claims that the Arbitration was over and Fiscal Funding was Judgment Proof.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Petitioners' motion to set aside judgments is denied. Although Petitioners' motion is plainly frivolous, solely to avoid further burden on the Court and the parties, Respondents' request that the Court issue an OSC re contempt is denied.

A trial court's power to grant such relief is governed by jurisdictional statutory limits. Motions to set aside a judgment must comply with the procedural requirements of Code of Civ. Proc. 663 and 663a and motions to set aside an order must comply with 1008. (Gariotti v. Hinkle (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 470, 479 ["California courts long have held section 660's deadline for a trial court to rule on a new trial motion is mandatory and jurisdictional."]; Kerns v. CSE Ins. Group (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 368, 391 ["[W]e hold that the procedural prerequisites set forth for reconsideration of orders and renewal of motions previously denied are jurisdictional."].) Petitioners' motions do not comply. Thus, there is no statutory basis for relief.

The judgments, which were affirmed by the Court of Appeal, are law of the case. That this Court declined to enjoin a pending arbitration that Respondent Grigsby re-filed after the AAA terminated the first-filed arbitration does not give rise to grounds to set aside the judgments. (see word minutes for full details)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
